files.00_Overview.md: Add Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways sections. The Overview is too brief - expand 'What are utilities?' (What), 'Why learn them?' (Why), and 'How will they help your workflow?' (How). Add concrete Prerequisites section.
files.01_Utility_Categories_and_Organization.m: Add Learning Objectives at the start. Content is comprehensive and well-structured with good 3W coverage. Consider adding Key Takeaways summary table at the end. The Exercises section is excellent - keep this format.
files.02_Architecture_and_Design_Patterns.md: Good depth on C++ architecture. Add Learning Objectives. The 'How' is strong but 'Why' needs strengthening - explain WHY understanding architecture matters before creating custom utilities. Add Key Takeaways.
files.03_Essential_Utilities_for_Common_CFD_Ta: CRITICAL: This file is severely underdeveloped compared to files 01 and 02. It's a brief summary when it should be a comprehensive guide. MASSIVE EXPANSION needed: add Learning Objectives, detailed explanations of each essential utility with What/Why/How for each, real examples, common pitfalls, exercises. This should be as detailed as file 01.
files.04_Time-Saving_Benefits.md: Excellent comparative analysis with before/after examples. Good motivation content. Add Learning Objectives. The 'Why' is very strong. Consider adding 'When to use utilities vs when to manual process' decision tree.
files.05_Creating_Custom_Utilities.md: CRITICAL: This file is TOO brief for such an important topic. It's a quick reference when it should be a comprehensive tutorial. MAJOR EXPANSION needed: add Learning Objectives, complete 3W framework (What is a custom utility, Why create one, How to build step-by-step), troubleshooting, common compilation errors, testing strategies, multiple examples from simple to advanced.
files.06_Integration_with_Solver_Workflows.md: Add Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways. Content is practical but needs more 'Why' - explain the benefits of integrated workflow vs ad-hoc approach. Add troubleshooting common integration issues.
files.07_Best_Practices.md: Add Learning Objectives. Good practical content but needs stronger 3W framework - explain WHAT constitutes a best practice, WHY each practice matters, and HOW to implement it properly. Add anti-patterns section (what NOT to do).

global_strategy: This folder has EXTREME quality inconsistency: files 01, 02, and 04 are comprehensive and well-structured, while files 03, 05, 06, and 07 are underdeveloped summaries. File 00 lacks proper 3W framework. The learning sequence is disrupted by these quality gaps. Priority: (1) Expand file 03 (Essential Utilities) to match file 01's detail level, (2) Expand file 05 (Custom Utilities) into comprehensive tutorial, (3) Strengthen files 00, 06, 07 with Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways, (4) Ensure all files follow consistent 3W Framework with clear What/Why/How structure. All files need standard sections: Learning Objectives at start, Key Takeaways at end, and proper link navigation.
